concept.title: Procedure
concept.shortdesc: The Inspection Supervisor shall be identified in each plant.

li.p: The Inspection Supervisor shall be identified in each plant.

ul.li: Accountable for the personnel who perform tasks on inspection equipment.
Facilitates planning, coordination, execution, documentation, communication, and continuous improvement of the inspection process in the plant.
The Inspection Supervisors’ responsibilities may or may not all be fulfilled by one individual, but each responsibility shall have a specific owner assigned.

Inspection related tasks may cross departments, such as job change, maintenance, production, and quality. In such a case, each department must have a nominated person that is accountable for all inspection related tasks. The person responsible for each task must be clearly documented, either in a job description or another format.
It is recommended to have one person named in the role of Inspection Supervisor for all inspection related responsibilities in the plant.

li.p: The Inspection Plant Network Leader shall be identified in each plant.

ul.li: Ensures responsibilities are understood as specified in Procedure MOE-02-043 Plant Network Leader Roles and Responsibilities.

ol.li: The Backup Inspection Plant Network Leader shall be identified in each plant.
The Inspection Supervisor shall verify all Inspection Personnel are in compliance with all safety requirements according to Procedure INS-02-001 Inspection Safety.
The Inspection Supervisor shall ensure all Inspection Personnel are properly trained for their specific responsibilities according to Procedure INS-02-010 Inspection Personnel Training.

li.p: The Inspection Supervisor shall ensure Inspection Personnel are following established requirements for the following

ol.li: The Inspection Supervisor shall be responsible for ensuring the plant is equipped properly and running at proper speeds in accordance with Procedure INS-02-080 Installed Inspection Equipment
The Inspection Supervisor shall ensure all Inspection Personnel are equipped with the proper tools to perform each task.
The Inspection Supervisor shall own the inspection process and implement improvements.
The Inspection Supervisor shall monitor Production Information Systems for machine performance and ensure issues are addressed.
The Inspection Supervisor shall ensure job history, settings, and operations meet the quality and operational requirements.
The Inspection Supervisor shall ensure adequate and correct spare equipment is available.
The Inspection Supervisor shall ensure adequate job change parts for all jobs on the schedule are available.
The Inspection Supervisor shall monitor the results of the challenge sample operation and ensure corrective actions are addressed.

li.p: The Inspection Supervisor shall maintain good communication and teamwork with Inspection Personnel and all departments.

ul.li: Inspection performance with Production and Maintenance Personnel
Quality performance with Plant Quality Personnel
Production plan and Job Change Strategy with Job Change Personnel
Training plan with Human Resources Personnel

ol.li: The Inspection Supervisor shall ensure proper housekeeping tasks are being performed regularly in all inspection areas, including workshop area(s).
